category_title: Sixth-grade students with diverse learning and emotional disorders
minimal_context: John is sitting at his desk during standard English language arts instruction and is expected to read the assigned text.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's continue with our standard English language arts instruction. Please read the next paragraph.

STUDENT: (Remains usually quiet and looks confused.)

TUTOR: What do you think is happening in this part of the text?

STUDENT: (Puts his head on the desk, remaining typically withdrawn.)

TUTOR: John, please sit up and look at the page.

STUDENT: (Looks confused again and begins to 'float' away, requiring constant 'wake-up' signals from the teacher.)

options.A: Tell the student, 'Don't say you can't, you can,' during the assigned reading activity.
options.B: Start the student out in teams of two to write each scene of the text.
options.C: Allow the student to withdraw from the environment for a time-limited period of quiet time.
options.D: Provide the student with freedom and autonomy during the assigned reading activity.
